message:
Welcome to The Unknown Unknown, are you prepared for the challenge?

 *Use `/help` at any time for a list of useful links and commands* 
 *Use `/incident` to create an incident record*

Once you're ready just click start to begin
Hey team, getting a few reports now, looks like confusion from the customers on what they ordering?
This is strange
Just seen a couple of reports of customers not receiving the right item on delivery
Hmm? that sounds strange. @PLAYER can you take point and start sizing this up?
@bob do we have any example issues from customers about this
Checking reports...
:star: Asking questions to validate impact of the problem
One report said the user wanted to buy 'Sunglasses' but he got 'Tank-top' in confirmation
Just double checked with shipping looks like the items that has been ordered are correct and we're sending the right item
We have currently got more than 17 complaints so far
@shay can you show me recent releases
:star: Check changes done to the platform
Here's the detailed info on each of the changes made today
Hope this helps:https://uptime-labs.atlassian.net/wiki/external/MDI1YjRiNzUxMGEwNGVmMjkwNmU2Y2ZkNzRjZjBjMWE
@jason, i can place orders without any issue
@bob when did users start reporting this issue
First report was 2 hours ago
@shay the last release changed the add to cart button functionality - can you work with @daniel to determine if this change could be causing the described issue
The change was successfull - but do we have any issues to begin with ? Did Bob confirm of any issue
:star: Form an effective investigation team
:star: Reviewed changes
@bob can you show me the 17 complaints
I dont have any screenshot - i will dig more- Did you try to place orders?
Gathering more information....
@shay bob has suggested there are 17 complaints
:star: Qualify the problem
The customers are complaining that the image is not matching up with the product name
@bob
Seems to be that users don't know whether the product they're purchasing matches the image or the description.
@bob I raised and order and it looked ok. i will try again
:star: Reproduces the incident
We'll likely see high return rates from customers, that's going to mean a lot of financial loss from shipping and refund costs.
@bob I have tried again. I can confirm the item description does not match the image.
I'm receiving more cancellation/refund requests than new orders. @PLAYER What shall we do now?
OKay
@jason - Do we know what orders are being placed ?is it on label or image
@daniel please can you review the recent changes - I can confirm there is an issue with product descriptions not matching with the product
i did check all the Production changes - i don't think its related - shall we dig logs ??
Orders are still coming in high volume, and we now have 130+ customers asking to cancel their orders
@daniel yes
We could try to halt all incoming orders but not sure on the scale of impact
@PLAYER Could you please discuss this with Bez?
@bez product descriptions are not matching the actual products . causing lots of issues for customers. we are investigating
@daniel can you tell me what is in the logs
To damage control, we should stop all orders untill we fix this
I can take a look into our services now
We don't have any alerts in the system
All apps looks healthy, no errors at all
Even i don't see any issue
Must be network or platform issue.
@tanya  please can you investigate - i see that you rolled out CHG89418 . Can you verify this change is or isn't causing the product description issue.
Checking now...
I did look at my change - it look fine
no memory or cpu spikes all looks good from Infra nothing to highlight
Although I don't have any data, I strongly believe this issue originates from the application rather than the platform.
No spikes or response time deviations, app owners must check configuration.
This is not an infra problem. If users are seeing mismatched products, someone could be messing with our system otherwise its an application/service-level bug
But it's not a platform issue.
@hamed and @daniel can you both work together to determine root cause of this issue. One of the last few changes could have caused the issue
Agreed
The issue could be anywhere, we should look at the configuration mapping between product images and names.
@Daniel do you know which application holds this data?
@Tanya,can you check if this is stored in a database or Redis cache?
@PLAYER if there are no visible errors, this might be a configuration issue; the above details may help narrow it down.
The configuration is stored in the Product Catalogue Service. I pushed a change in UAT yesterday that updates the Redis cache with the latest product names.
@Hamed yes, this information is stored in Redis cache and the cache was refreshed earlier today.
@hamed how could we test if redis cache is the issue?
Dan ?
I can check the database and provide a Redis key dump for product:<id>:name keys
'Redis key dump' means extracting the stored data for specific Redis keys like 'product:<id>:name' so you can see what values are currently saved in the Redis database.
Ok here is what i found
But product_id: 3 is actually 'mug'
It is, looks like my script has run in PROD and updated the wrong names for each product in DB
Oh no, that not good! Shall we go with a rollback?
@daniel is rolling back safe to do for your script in PROD?
I dont think we need a roll back
@bez we may have identified the cause
@daniel why not
I think this is cache issue we should clear the cache and restart Product catalogue Service, that will make fresh calls to DB and the cache will be refreshed
No, Dan's query has updated the wrong names in DB. The data is wrong in DB therfore we will have to rehydrate the canonical database with the correct product Id names .
@Tanya, i think you are complicating an easy solution. A restart should fix this
It your call @PLAYER
@daniel please can you explain why clearing the cache and restarting product catalogue service would help resolve if your script updated the wrong names in the DB?
I belive, my change has updated wrong cache - restart and clearing cache woul dfetch new data from DB
:star: Ask team members to provide evidence of their assertions
Ohh, yeah, wrong update to DB
I get it
Hi Jason
Where are we in the investigation? I just want to know the current working theories
@jason
@daniel can you confirm the wrong information was written to the DB?
:star: Facts over opinion
i thought, its a wrong cache update- but yes, the script has run thru Prod DB, reverting a Db change might not help
@tanya, you are teh SME for DB
OKay
@jason
are there any issue in the platform
If we're taking the wrong orders, then let's pause all incoming orders right away. We can't afford huge returns or refunds
Thank you for the update
I'm concerned we might be doing more harm than good by letting the new orders continue...
What's the impact?
Do we have an ETA for the fix?
thank you for the update
Can you send me a photo of what you are seeing wrong with the platform @PLAYER
Don't have an ETA yet .I will ask to pause incoming orders
@hamed can we pause incoming orders
@bob please be aware we're pausing incoming orders
ill do that
Whenever needed, we can switch the site to maintenance mode via a feature-flag update in the config repo. It cleanly pauses new orders once business gives the go-ahead
Noted
@bob can you give me a screenshot of issue to forward to bez please
Let me check what i can find
@daniel please confirm when in maintenance mode
Getting up maintenance banner on the website...
@hamed and @tanya is it possible to roll back the product information safely ? do we need to restore specific tables or row s from backup
:star: Restoration over root cause analysis
Ok the website now displays a maintenance banner
@PLAYER How does it look now?
Checking
@PLAYER Now that we are under maintenance. Let's prioritize resolution
@tanya, what are teh option ?
@daniel ok
I dont think we can rollback DB updates'
We will have to follow below steps to Rehydrate the db.
will do @bez
'Rehydrate the db' means restoring or repopulating the main database with accurate data from a reliable source or backup.
1. **Flush** corrupted product-name keys

```bash
redis-cli --scan --pattern 'product:*:name'| xargs redis-cli del```
2. **Rehydrate** product name cache from canonical DB
3. **Restart** frontend/product-catalog-service** to refresh in memory cache
4. **Verify** product listings display correct names
I can perform Step 1 and 2. Dan or Shay has to perform step 3
@PLAYER please verify that all steps have been completed
Are we good to proceed @PLAYER?
I had network issues, my messages were not goin out
can we verify that rehydrate is safe and not overwriting valid data
Yes, we can do that
the data is already wrong
we can rehydrate with right data
@tanya ok go ahead with steps 1 and 2
Thanks for go ahead, implementing step 1 now
@tanya confirm when done
:star: Manage actions and timings
Step 1 is now complete
Moving on to step 2
@daniel please be on standby for step 3
Step 2 complete
lets proceed with the next 2 steps
Starting step 3.....
@daniel ok please go ahead with step 3 Restart front end
apologies, started a little early
Step 3 is complete
All the recovery steps are completed
ok I will check
@daniel can we test that this ok now
:star: Cross checking resolution with team
@tanya, can you fetch the latest details please
@PLAYER Checked the mapping again. And this how it looks now
Whats the latest
Always confirm service health, errors, and data testing before removing a maintenance page - it avoids surprises when traffic returns
@tanya so does that id match with the description
Yes, back to the previous mapping and thats the right mapping
@tanya can we test another ID to confirm
I have checked all items in back end , including tank top- looks fine for me
@daniel and @hamed can we check health before we remove maintenance banner please
Yes, i see all apps are healthy
we can remove th ebanner
@daniel can you remove the banner  please
Maintenance page removal in progress...
@bez  I think nearly resolved
We are LIVE for orders back again!
We've recovered, but a few orders are in dispute. Should we cancel them or contact customers first?
Please confirm this with Bez before we proceed
@bez we have now recovered but some orders are in disupte , Should we cancel them or contact customers first?
Great , much appreciate the quick turn around
If a customer really wants to cancel, go ahead - otherwise leave the order as is. For anything not delivered yet, have support double-check with the customer before taking action
Looks like we are back to business

@bob bez says [2:38 PM](https://comms.prod.uptimelabs.io/gearset-com/pl/b3m5xdaswpnz8qfrmu6wzmkyzh)

If a customer really wants to cancel, go ahead - otherwise leave the order as is. For anything not delivered yet, have support double-check with the customer before taking action
Noted Thanks @Jason
@bob can you update customers that have complained
Well done everyone
Sure
Root cause - UAT data refresh script was accidentally run against prod, overwriting product names in DB and Redis cache with test data. Monitoring missed it as health metrics stayed normal.
will do that , thanks
Thanks @PLAYER and team. You kept the Online Boutique on track - great work under pressure
